prompt: Who did Novak Djokovic beat in the 2023 French Open final to complete a career Grand Slam sweep by then?
explanation: Djokovic beat Casper Ruud in straight sets to win his third French Open title in 2023.

prompt: Which player did Novak Djokovic beat in an epic five-set 2019 Wimbledon final, considered one of the greatest matches ever?
explanation: Djokovic beat Federer in a match tiebreak in the fifth set, saving championship points along the way.

prompt: Which unique tiebreak rule was introduced for the first time at the 2019 Wimbledon final's deciding set?
explanation: Wimbledon introduced a final-set tiebreak at 12-12 starting in 2019, first used in the Djokovic-Federer final.

prompt: Who won the legendary 2008 Wimbledon final, often cited as the greatest tennis match ever played?
explanation: Nadal beat Federer 9-7 in the fifth set in fading light, ending Federer's five-year Wimbledon reign.

prompt: What was the final set score of the epic 2008 Wimbledon final between Nadal and Federer?
explanation: Nadal won the deciding fifth set 9-7 in near darkness at Wimbledon.

prompt: Which player won the longest match in Grand Slam history, an 11-hour epic against John Isner at Wimbledon 2010?
explanation: John Isner beat Nicolas Mahut 70-68 in the fifth set, the longest match in tennis history.

prompt: What was the final set score of the famous Isner-Mahut match at Wimbledon 2010?
explanation: The fifth set alone lasted over 8 hours, ending 70-68 in Isner's favor.

prompt: Which player retired from tennis in 2022 after an emotional Laver Cup appearance?
explanation: Roger Federer's final professional match came at the 2022 Laver Cup in London.

prompt: Which player announced his retirement from tennis after the 2024 season, ending an era for the 'Big Three'?
explanation: Rafael Nadal retired in November 2024, following an emotional Davis Cup farewell.

prompt: Which player won the 2022 Wimbledon women's singles title?
explanation: Elena Rybakina won her first Grand Slam title, beating Ons Jabeur in the 2022 final.

prompt: Which player won the 2023 Wimbledon women's singles title?
explanation: Unseeded Marketa Vondrousova upset the field to win the 2023 Wimbledon title.

prompt: Which player won the Australian Open women's singles title in 2023 and 2024?
explanation: Aryna Sabalenka won back-to-back Australian Open titles in 2023 and 2024.

prompt: Which country is Aryna Sabalenka from?
explanation: Aryna Sabalenka is from Belarus and rose to world No.1 in the mid-2020s.

prompt: Which player won four consecutive French Open women's titles between 2020 and 2024?
explanation: Iga Swiatek dominated the French Open, winning multiple titles including a streak in the 2020s.

prompt: Which player retired suddenly at world No.1 in 2022, surprising the tennis world?
explanation: Ashleigh Barty retired in March 2022 while ranked world No.1, shortly after winning the Australian Open.

prompt: Which Japanese player won the US Open in both 2018 and 2020?
explanation: Naomi Osaka won the 2018 and 2020 US Open titles, becoming a global star.

prompt: Which player did Naomi Osaka beat in the controversial 2018 US Open final?
explanation: Osaka beat Serena Williams in a final marked by controversy over code violations against Williams.

prompt: Which player won an Olympic gold medal in tennis singles at the 2016 Rio Games?
explanation: Andy Murray defended his Olympic title, winning gold at both London 2012 and Rio 2016.

prompt: Which British player won Wimbledon in 2013, ending a 77-year wait for a British men's singles champion?
explanation: Andy Murray became the first British man to win Wimbledon since Fred Perry in 1936.

prompt: Which player did Andy Murray beat in the 2013 Wimbledon final?
explanation: Murray beat Djokovic in straight sets to end Britain's long wait for a Wimbledon men's champion.

prompt: Which player won three Grand Slam titles in a single season (2015) and is known for his big serve?
explanation: Djokovic won the Australian Open, Wimbledon, and US Open in 2015, missing only the French Open.

prompt: Which player beat Djokovic in the 2015 French Open final, denying him a calendar Grand Slam bid that year?
explanation: Stan Wawrinka's brutal one-handed backhand carried him past Djokovic in the 2015 French Open final.

prompt: How many Grand Slam titles has Stan Wawrinka won in his career?
explanation: Wawrinka won the 2014 Australian Open, 2015 French Open, and 2016 US Open.

prompt: Which player did Stan Wawrinka beat in the 2016 US Open final?
explanation: Wawrinka beat Djokovic in four sets to win his third and final Grand Slam title.

prompt: Which Croatian player won the 2014 US Open, beating Kei Nishikori in the final?
explanation: Marin Cilic won his only Grand Slam title at the 2014 US Open.

prompt: Which player famously never won a Grand Slam title despite reaching multiple finals, nicknamed the best player never to win a major?
options: All are frequently cited in this discussion
explanation: Ferrer, Nishikori, and Berdych are all commonly cited as top players who never won a Grand Slam singles title.

prompt: Which player won the Olympic gold medal in men's singles tennis at the 2020 Tokyo Olympics?
explanation: Alexander Zverev won gold in Tokyo, beating Karen Khachanov in the final.

prompt: Which player won the men's singles gold medal at the 2024 Paris Olympics?
explanation: Djokovic won his first Olympic gold medal at the 2024 Paris Games, beating Alcaraz in the final.

prompt: Which two players contested the men's singles final at the 2024 Paris Olympics?
explanation: Novak Djokovic beat Carlos Alcaraz in straight sets to win Olympic gold in Paris.

prompt: Which player has won the WTA Finals (season-ending championship) the most times?
options: Both Navratilova and Graf multiple times
explanation: Martina Navratilova and Steffi Graf both won the WTA season-ending championship multiple times during their careers.

prompt: Which player famously fought back from two sets down to win the 2017 Australian Open final at age 35?

prompt: Which coach guided Novak Djokovic for much of his most dominant period, a former Grand Slam champion himself?
options: All have coached Djokovic at different times
explanation: Djokovic has worked with Marian Vajda for most of his career, plus Boris Becker and Goran Ivanisevic at various points.

prompt: Which coach is most associated with guiding Rafael Nadal throughout the majority of his career?
explanation: Toni Nadal coached his nephew Rafael for the majority of his early and prime career.

explanation: Nadal won 9-7 in the fifth set in fading light, ending Federer's five-year reign.

statement: Rafael Nadal retired from professional tennis in 2024.

prompt: Which player announced his retirement from professional tennis in September 2022, with his farewell match at the Laver Cup?
explanation: Federer's retirement was announced in September 2022, and he played his final match in doubles at the Laver Cup alongside Nadal.

explanation: David Ferrer reached his only Grand Slam final at the 2013 French Open, losing to Nadal.

prompt: Since which Olympics has mixed doubles been part of the Olympic tennis program?
explanation: Mixed doubles returned to the Olympic tennis program starting at the 2012 London Games.

explanation: Andreescu beat Serena Williams in straight sets to win her first Grand Slam title.

prompt: Which player won the 2020 Australian Open women's singles title?

prompt: Which player beat Serena Williams in the 2019 Wimbledon final?
explanation: Simona Halep beat Serena Williams in a dominant straight-sets final at Wimbledon 2019.

prompt: Which surface do most ATP Masters 1000 events, aside from Monte Carlo, Madrid, and Rome, get played on?
explanation: Most Masters 1000 events, such as Indian Wells and Miami, are played on hard courts.

prompt: Which of these Masters 1000 tournaments is played on clay?
explanation: The Monte Carlo Masters is a clay-court event, part of the European clay swing.

prompt: Which term describes when a lower-ranked player defeats a much higher-ranked player unexpectedly?
explanation: An 'upset' occurs when a lower-ranked or unseeded player beats a higher-ranked favorite.

prompt: Which player set the record for most consecutive weeks at world No.1 in the ATP rankings?
explanation: Roger Federer held the world No.1 ranking for 237 consecutive weeks, a long-standing record.

prompt: Which player surpassed Roger Federer's record for total weeks (not necessarily consecutive) spent at world No.1?
explanation: Novak Djokovic has spent more total weeks at world No.1 than any other player in ATP history.

prompt: Which player won a record number of year-end world No.1 finishes in the ATP rankings?
explanation: Djokovic has finished as year-end world No.1 more times than any other player.

prompt: Which American player won a record six year-end No.1 finishes in the 1990s?
explanation: Pete Sampras finished as year-end world No.1 for six consecutive years in the 1990s.

prompt: Which player won 14 Grand Slam titles during his career, a record before Federer, Nadal, and Djokovic surpassed it?
explanation: Pete Sampras's 14 Grand Slam titles stood as the men's record until the 2000s.

prompt: Which player completed a career Grand Slam (all four majors at different points) in the 1990s, known for his all-court game?
explanation: Andre Agassi won all four Grand Slam tournaments during his career, completing the career Grand Slam.

prompt: Which player married Andre Agassi and is herself a Grand Slam champion?
explanation: Steffi Graf married Andre Agassi in 2001; both are among tennis's greatest champions.

prompt: Which player was stabbed by a spectator during a changeover in 1993, a shocking incident in tennis history?
explanation: Monica Seles was stabbed by an obsessed fan of Steffi Graf in 1993, derailing her career momentum.

prompt: Which player won the men's singles title at the first-ever US Open Grand Slam in 1968 (start of the Open Era)?
explanation: Arthur Ashe won the first US Open of the Open Era in 1968.

prompt: Which player became the first Black man to win a Grand Slam singles title?
explanation: Arthur Ashe made history winning the 1968 US Open, becoming the first Black man to win a major.

prompt: Which player became the first Black woman to win a Grand Slam singles title, at the 1956 French Championships?
explanation: Althea Gibson broke barriers by winning the French Championships in 1956, followed by Wimbledon and the US Championships.

prompt: Which French player won the French Open at home in 1983, becoming a national hero known for his flamboyant style?
explanation: Yannick Noah remains the last Frenchman to win the men's singles title at Roland Garros.

prompt: Which player is the only man to win the Wimbledon, French Open, Australian Open, and US Open titles as an amateur and then again as a professional (double Grand Slam) in 1962 and 1969?
explanation: Rod Laver achieved the rare feat of two calendar Grand Slams, in 1962 (as an amateur) and 1969 (as a pro).

prompt: Which player won a record 12 Australian Open men's singles titles?
explanation: Djokovic has won a record 10 Australian Open titles as of the mid-2020s, more than any other player at that Slam.

prompt: How many Australian Open men's singles titles has Novak Djokovic won as of 2024?
explanation: Djokovic won his 10th Australian Open title in 2023, extending his record at the tournament.

prompt: Which player famously said 'You cannot be serious!' to an umpire during a heated Wimbledon match in 1981?
explanation: John McEnroe's fiery outburst became one of tennis's most famous quotes.

prompt: Which player is known for his intense rivalry with Bjorn Borg in the late 1970s and early 1980s?
explanation: The Borg-McEnroe rivalry, including their epic 1980 Wimbledon final, is legendary in tennis history.

prompt: What was the tiebreak score in the famous fourth set of the 1980 Wimbledon final between Borg and McEnroe?
explanation: McEnroe won a marathon fourth-set tiebreak 18-16, though Borg won the match in five sets.

prompt: Which player won five consecutive Wimbledon titles from 1976 to 1980, before losing to McEnroe in 1981?
explanation: Bjorn Borg's five consecutive Wimbledon titles stood as a record for decades.

prompt: Which player retired from professional tennis at just 25 years old, at the height of his career, in 1983?
explanation: Borg's early retirement shocked the tennis world, as he was still one of the sport's top players.

statement: Arthur Ashe was the first Black man to win a Grand Slam singles title.
explanation: Ashe won the inaugural US Open of the Open Era in 1968.

statement: Monica Seles was stabbed by a spectator during a match in 1993.
explanation: The attack, by an obsessed fan of rival Steffi Graf, derailed years of Seles's career.

statement: Pete Sampras completed a career Grand Slam by winning all four majors.
explanation: Sampras never won the French Open, missing out on the career Grand Slam despite his 14 major titles.

statement: Rod Laver completed the calendar Grand Slam twice, in 1962 and 1969.
explanation: Laver's 1969 Slam came during the Open Era, making it especially notable.

statement: John McEnroe and Bjorn Borg had one of the most famous rivalries in tennis history.
explanation: Their 1980 Wimbledon final, including an 18-16 fourth-set tiebreak, is legendary.
